commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No concrete repair remains; maintainers should complete ordinary exact-head CI gating and merge review.; Cleared: The patch adds no dependency or supply-chain surface and improves credential hygiene by refusing to transmit locally expired bearer tokens while retaining cookie-only fallback. (sha=40d58c6031f575425dbcba7d08ac2485c6c4727a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR combines non-expired Grok CLI bearer credentials with browser-cookie attempts, recognizes credential-related status-7 responses, parses framed or raw billing protobufs including omitted zero use, improves reauthentication guidance, and adds focused tests and docs.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main lacks bearer-plus-cookie attempts and raw/omitted-zero handling, focused fixtures cover each boundary, and the exact-head live provider run demonstrates the repaired result.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No concrete repair remains; maintainers should complete ordinary exact-head CI gating and merge review.

target.title: Fix Grok web billing status 7 auth and cookie bearer fetch
target.body:
## Summary

- Treat gRPC status 7 OAuth2 `bad-credentials` responses as auth failures without misclassifying scope-denied responses.
- Try browser cookies plus a non-expired bearer token before cookie-only; never transmit expired bearer credentials.
- Accept complete gRPC-web frames and raw protobuf billing responses, including current periods where proto3 omits a zero `credit_usage_percent`.
- Surface actionable grok.com or `grok login` reauthentication guidance.

## Problem

Grok web billing could fail with RPC status 7 even when the user had a current Chrome grok.com session and/or a usable CLI token. Successful billing responses also vary between framed and raw protobuf, and zero usage may omit the scalar percent field.

## Current candidate

Exact head: `40d58c6031f575425dbcba7d08ac2485c6c4727a`, rebased without conflicts onto current `main` at `9b22e65b`.

Exact-head proof:
- Focused tests: 55 tests in 3 suites passed.
- `make check`: 0 violations across 1,066 files.
- Release build and debug app packaging: passed.
- Signed-app deep code-sign verification: passed.
- Exact app executable SHA-256: `7816fdbdc243b4b13cfb8ac57e93437c302f1aeb6f4b55a800a62db172abbbb9`.
- Real provider/account verification used the available Chrome grok.com session in the exact built app. The menu showed Grok Credits at `100% left`, reset in `18d 20h`, and `Updated just now`.
- Safe live-proof crop SHA-256: `bc89c635ff92414696ab72ddf89f752a778c9097e395ab4818bb503dac5c4318`.
- Autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings (`gpt-5.5`, confidence 0.82).
- Public Model Identifier Gate: PASS. The only identifier in verification logs was `gpt-5.5`, confirmed in [official OpenAI model documentation](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5.5); the built artifact had 0 candidate-only identifiers.
- ClawSweeper exact-head review: high confidence, proof sufficient, security cleared, no repair lane.
- Fresh exact-head CI pending.

## Full-suite context

Two local exact-head full-suite attempts reached 3,790 tests but encountered timing flakes outside this PR's five-file diff: the new `MainThreadHangWatchdogTests` from current `main`, and existing Codex RPC stub timeout tests. The affected suites pass in isolation: 2 watchdog tests and 22 Codex tests. Exact-head CI remains the authoritative full gate.

## Test plan

- `swift test --filter 'GrokWebBillingFetcherTests|GrokAuthTests|CodexUserFacingErrorTests'`
- `swift test`
- `make check`
- `swift build -c release`
- signed-app live verification against grok.com
target.author: bcharleson

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
